old_claim.note: Shamanism & Animism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because the corrected node is anchored to a Blombos abstract ochre drawing and graphic-symbol tradition, not to a documented shamanic mediator or generic animistic belief system.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: reports a cross-hatched ochre drawing from approximately 73,000-year-old Blombos Cave levels and discusses graphic designs and modern cognition without identifying shamanism, animism, or ritual mediation as a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports early abstract graphic marking but not a shamanism or animism dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping the edge would launder a broad ritual-belief interpretation into an archaeological graphic-marking claim.

ontology_before: Early Symbolic Communication depended directly on Shamanism & Animism through a generic expert-inference edge.
ontology_after: Early Symbolic Communication is scoped to source-backed abstract graphic marks; shamanism and animism remain separate belief-system nodes for cases with direct ritual or spirit-mediation evidence.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: shamanism_animism is no longer a direct prerequisite for early_symbolic_communication.
would_reject_if: A Blombos source directly identified shamanic mediation as required for the abstract drawing or engraved ochre tradition.
The node were intentionally broadened to ritual-symbolic systems rather than graphic signs.
Downstream nodes required a shamanism path through early_symbolic_communication.
short_reason: The reviewed Blombos evidence documents abstract graphic marking, not a shamanic or animistic prerequisite.
